div.p: Message: Invalid argument supplied for foreach()

verStmt.version: v2.0: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes:
Version 1 was accessed by DataFirst on the 22nd of March 2021. It didn't contain covid variables.
Version 2 was received by DataFirst from Stats SA on the 16th of July 2021. It contains the 9 covid variables and is otherwise the same as version 1.

stdyInfo.abstract: The Quarterly Labour Force Survey (QLFS) is a household-based sample survey conducted by Statistics South Africa (Stats SA). It collects data on the labour market activities of individuals aged 15 years or older who live in South Africa.

sumDscr.universe: The QLFS sample covers the non-institutional population of South Africa with one exception. The only institutional subpopulation included in the QLFS sample are individuals in worker's hostels. Persons living in private dwelling units within institutions are also enumerated. For example, within a school compound, one would enumerate the schoolmaster's house and teachers' accommodation because these are private dwellings. Students living in a dormitory on the school compound would, however, be excluded.

stdyInfo.notes:
For Q4 2020 Statistics South Africa contiued to use Computer Assisted Telephone Interviewing, in an attempt to reduce the spread of COVID-19. This meant that households without telephones were out of scope. See the statistical release for more information.
INDIVIDUALS: labour market activity, labour preferences, labour market history, demographic characteristics, marital status, employment status, education, grants, tax.
From 2010 the income data collected by South Africa's Quarterly Labour Force Survey is no longer provided in the QLFS dataset (except for a brief return in QLFS 2010 Q3 which may be an error). Possibly because the data is unreliable at the level of the quarter, Statistics South Africa now provides the income data from the QLFS in an annualised dataset called Labour Market Dynamics in South Africa (LMDSA). The datasets for LMDSA are available from DataFirst's website.

dataColl.sampProc: The samping procedure for this quarter of the QLFS had to be changed from the norm due to covid-19. In this 2020 Q4 the sample used was the same sample as from Q3, Q2 and Q1 from 2020. Because the interviews were done telephonically, any sampling units that did not have telephones dropped out of the sample. This was adjusted for in the weighting procedure.
dataColl.weight:
Because the survey was done telehponically, households without telephones were essentially out of scope. To account for this problem,weights were "bias-adjusted" by Statistics South Africa. There was also significant imputation done to try and make the data more representative, despite covid.
For more details see section 4 of the stastical release and the metadata document.
dataColl.cleanOps: In general, imputation is used for item non-response (i.e. blanks within the questionnaire) and edit failures (i.e. invalid or inconsistent responses).

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. Quarterly Labour Force Survey 2020: Q4 [dataset]. Version 2. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2021. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2021. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/gd4d-s714
useStmt.conditions: Public access data, available to all

catgry.labl: Living together like husband and wife
Bachelors Degree and Post Graduate Diploma
Pre-school (including day care; crèche; Grade R and Pre-Grade R in an ECD Centre
Ordinary School (Including Grade R Learners Who Attend a Formal School; Grade 1-
Adult basic Education and Training Centre (ABET CENTRE)

var.name: Q25APDWRK
var.labl: Have paid work to return to
var.imputation: Have paid work to return to
var.security: Have paid work to return to
var.embargo: Have paid work to return to
var.respUnit: Have paid work to return to

var.name: Q25BOWNBUSNS
var.labl: Have own business to return to
var.imputation: Have own business to return to
var.security: Have own business to return to
var.embargo: Have own business to return to
var.respUnit: Have own business to return to

var.name: Q25CUNPDWRK
var.labl: Have an unpaid work to return to
var.imputation: Have an unpaid work to return to
var.security: Have an unpaid work to return to
var.embargo: Have an unpaid work to return to
var.respUnit: Have an unpaid work to return to

catgry.labl: Caring for family or others(Except Maternity/Paternity leave)
Temporarily laid off/Reduction in economic activity
Start a new job/business at a definite date in the future

var.name: Q33HAVEJOB
var.labl: Have a job or start a business
var.imputation: Have a job or start a business
var.security: Have a job or start a business
var.embargo: Have a job or start a business
var.respUnit: Have a job or start a business

var.name: Q33AWHNSTART
var.labl: How soon are you expecting to start work or a business
var.imputation: How soon are you expecting to start work or a business
var.security: How soon are you expecting to start work or a business
var.embargo: How soon are you expecting to start work or a business
var.respUnit: How soon are you expecting to start work or a business

catgry.labl: In more than 1 month to 3 month
Retired or too old for work

var.name: Q36TIMESEEK
var.labl: How long been trying to find work
var.imputation: How long been trying to find work
var.security: How long been trying to find work
var.embargo: How long been trying to find work
var.respUnit: How long been trying to find work

var.name: Q38RSNNOTSEEK
var.labl: Reason why not look for work or start business
var.imputation: Reason why not look for work or start business
var.security: Reason why not look for work or start business
var.embargo: Reason why not look for work or start business
var.respUnit: Reason why not look for work or start business

catgry.labl: Waiting to be recalled to former job
Undergoing training to help find work
No jobs available in the area
Lack of money to pay for transport to look for work
Unable to find work requiring his/her skills
Lost hope of finding any kind of work

var.name: Q311bWHNSTART
var.labl: How soon can you start work or a business
var.imputation: How soon can you start work or a business
var.security: How soon can you start work or a business
var.embargo: How soon can you start work or a business
var.respUnit: How soon can you start work or a business

Own account worker (not employing any employees)
Helping without pay in a household business

Current work shift arrangements/work every other week

catgry.catValu: REDUCTION IN WORK DUE TO COVID PARENTS NOT SENDING CHILDREN TO CRECHE
lockdown restrictions no customers, no work
lockdown restrictions people still scared to make contact with others
